title: See Amid the Winter's Snow

T: See Amid the Winter's Snow

w: An- gels sing- ing 'Peace on earth' Told us of the Sa- vior's birth." 
w: Thus to come from high- est bliss Down to such a world as this.

% 18
W: 6.Teach, O teach us, holy Child,
W: By Thy face so meek and mild,
W: Teach us to resemble Thee,
W: In Thy sweet humility.

metadata.composer: 'Humility' John Goss, 1870 "The Children's Hymnal, with Tunes", 1875
metadata.copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2008 Revision.
c_lines: Words: Edward Caswall, 1851.  Music: 'Humility' John Goss, 1870.
copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2008 Revision.

1.raw_text: See amid the winter's snow, Born for us on earth below, See, the gentle Lamb appears, Promised from eternal years. Hail that ever blessèd morn, Hail redemption's happy dawn, Sing through all Jeru- salem: Christ is born in Bethlehem.

2.raw_text: Lo, within a manger lies He Who built the starry skies; He Who, thronèd in height sublime, Sits amid the cheru- bim. Hail that ever blessèd morn, Hail redemption's happy dawn, Sing through all Jeru- salem: Christ is born in Bethlehem.

3.raw_text: Say, you holy shepherds, say, Tell your joyful news today. Why have you now left your sheep On the lonely mountain steep? Hail that ever blessèd morn, Hail redemption's happy dawn, Sing through all Jeru- salem: Christ is born in Bethlehem.

4.raw_text: "As we watched at dead of night, Lo, we saw a wondrous light; Angels singing 'Peace on earth' Told us of the Savior's birth." Hail that ever blessèd morn, Hail redemption's happy dawn, Sing through all Jeru- salem: Christ is born in Bethlehem.

5.raw_text: Sacred Infant, all divine, What a tender love was Thine, Thus to come from highest bliss Down to such a world as this. Hail that ever blessèd morn, Hail redemption's happy dawn, Sing through all Jeru- salem: Christ is born in Bethlehem.
